original_abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are highly persistent environmental pollutants that pose significant risks to human and ecological health, even at ultratrace levels (ng·L⁻¹). Despite their prevalence, current detection methods rely on sophisticated laboratory equipment, precluding the possibility of real-time, on-site monitoring across distributed water infrastructures. To address this gap, we report the development of amplifying fluorescent polymers (AFPs) designed for the selective detection of perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) and perfluorooctane sulfonate (PFOS) at parts-per-trillion concentrations. These AFPs utilize poly(p-phenylene ethynylene) and polyfluorene backbones integrated with highly fluorinated domains and pyridine-based selectors. The fluorinated regions facilitate the partitioning of PFAS into the polymer matrix, where the acidic analytes engage in a proton-transfer reaction with the pyridine units. This interaction creates low-energy exciton traps, resulting in a distinct red-shift in the fluorescence emission. We evaluated the sensing performance of these AFPs in both thin-film and nanoparticle formats, achieving detection limits of approximately 1 ppb and 100 ppt, respectively. Notably, the sensing response remained robust across various water matrices, including Milli-Q, deionized, and well water. These findings establish a versatile and sensitive platform for the rapid, field-deployable detection of aqueous PFAS at environmentally relevant scales.
humanized_abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are widespread, persistent and known to have harmful health and environmental effects at ultratrace levels (ng·L−1). As a result, it is not currently feasible to monitor distributed water systems in real time in the field. Herein, we present amplifying fluorescent polymers (AFPs) for the sensitive, selective detection of the well-known PFAS contaminants, perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) and perfluorooctane sulfonate (PFOS) at the parts-per-trillion level. These AFPs consist of poly(p-phenylene ethynylene) and polyfluorene backbones functionalized with fluorinated domains and pyridine-based PFAS-selectors. The fluorinated domains enhance the hydrophobic interactions between the hydrophobic PFAS analytes and the polymer. Upon binding to the pendant pyridine selectors of the AFP, the protonated PFOA and PFOS undergo a proton-transfer reaction. The analyte--selector interaction produces an energy trap in the polymer excited-state leading to an emission shift to the red end of the spectrum. Our AFPs, as thin films and nanoparticles, exhibit detection limits of 1 ppb and 100 ppt, respectively. Moreover, the robustness of AFPs toward water matrix interference is confirmed via tests with samples of Milli-Q, deionized and well water. These results indicate the suitability of these AFPs for a field applicable, point-of-use (POU) platform that is able to quickly monitor PFAS contamination in the environment at environmentally relevant concentrations.

The fluorinated domains enhance the hydrophobic interactions between the hydrophobic PFAS analytes and the polymer.
